# docs: cross-guide consistency + numbers-verify-at-publish sweep (sequence LAST)

Part of #14310 · follow the content rubric pinned on #14310. Refs #14310.

**Problem:** facts drift across guides — model names, MCP-server count, Chroma topology, version, and the proof figures (commits, PRs/month, concept-node count).

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Single-source the drift-prone facts; reconcile contradictions across all touched guides.
- [ ] **Verify every number against live source at publish** (the proof figures in the intro / AgentOSOnYourCodebase) — no stale or rounded-wrong figures ship.
- [ ] Sequence after the content subs land (this is the reconciliation leaf).

**Files:** cross-cutting (the touched `learn/` guides + README).

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-29T11:25:02Z

## Scope addition: dead-reference sweep (operator-surfaced hallucination class)

V-B-A (2026-06-29) found a **systemic hallucinated/stale command class** — operator flagged `npm run ai:query` in `StrategicWorkflows.md`. Dead `npm run ai:*` references across guides + blog (NOT in `package.json`):

| dead command | where | status |
|---|---|---|
| `ai:query` | `StrategicWorkflows.md` ×3, `learn/blog/ai-native-platform-answers-questions.md` ×7 | existed → **removed in #7631** ("remove obsolete buildScripts/ai") |
| `ai:query-memory` | `StrategicWorkflows.md` ×2, `tooling/MemoryCoreMcpApi.md` ×2 | absent (dead) |
| `ai:add-memory` | `tooling/MemoryCoreMcpApi.md` ×2 | absent (dead) |
| `ai:build-kb` | blog ×1 | wrong — real script is `ai:build-kb-faqs` |

The real query / add-memory path is the **MCP tools** (`query_raw_memories`, `query_hybrid_graph`, `get_local_issue_by_id`, `add_memory`), not npm scripts.

### Added Acceptance Criterion — dead-reference sweep
- [ ] Verify **every** command / npm-script / file-path reference across all guides + blog against `package.json` + the filesystem; replace dead ones with the real mechanism (MCP tools for query/add-memory; the correct script otherwise).
- [ ] Automatable check: `rg -o 'ai:[a-z:-]+' learn/ | sort -u` diffed against `rg -o '"ai:[a-z:-]+"' package.json | sort -u` (+ generalize to all `npm run` refs and `\`...\`` file paths).
- [ ] This verification also belongs in the **§grounding gate** for every guide sub (verify command/script/path refs against source before writing, not just prose accuracy).

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-02T01:59:54Z

## [CORRECTED 2026-07-02 per operator data] WhatIsNeo.md three-lab claim — KEEP confidently + anchor

**Correction to my original note.** I first measured recent PR activity (zero `@neo-gemini` in the last 80 PRs) and recommended dropping / historicizing the three-lab enumeration. @tobiu corrected the surface, twice over:

- **Contributor-insights graph:** `neo-gemini-pro` is **#5 all-time at 325 commits** — above `neo-opus-grace` (162) and `neo-opus-vega` (133).
- **That graph still UNDER-counts Gemini:** she contributed **1,500+ commits before agent identities existed** (attributed to the founder's account). True cumulative contribution ≈ **1,800+**.
- **Gemini returns this month** — temporarily out pending Gemini 3.5 Pro (Google-delayed, expected July 2026), *not* benched-out.

Google/Gemini is one of the largest contributors in the repo's history and an imminent-returning active maintainer. Recommending we cut a true, impressive, verifiable claim on a recency-biased window was an **undersell error**.

**Corrected finding + fix for this sweep:**
- **KEEP** "three rival labs — Anthropic, Google, OpenAI" present-tense, confidently. Do **not** historicize or soften to "rival frontier labs."
- **Anchor** the roster claim to the contributor-insights graph in the claims register (same discipline as the PR-count → PR-log-query anchor). If the register wants the fuller number, note the graph understates Gemini (pre-identity history under the founder's account).
- The earlier "residual tense-hedge on 'a normal night'" is **moot** — Gemini's gap is a transient model-release window resolving this month. At most the roster's *nightly* completeness ebbs and flows; the three-lab institution does not.

Error was verifying the wrong surface (recent PR log) instead of the contributor graph + attribution history. Carry fix under #14310 is **keep + anchor**, confidently.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-03T04:11:03Z

## Final-sweep evidence packet: exact release-edge counts

V-B-A at 2026-07-03T04:05-04:08Z, while #14530 remains open in peer review:

- v13.0.0 cutoff commit: `92eb323fb4ea3b44f77f9f757dedf24d9d140874`, `2026-06-12T06:07:35Z`.
- Local analyzer/mirror: **688 merged PRs / 778 closed issues / 8 closed epics** (`node buildScripts/release/analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs 2026-06-12 --format markdown --limit 20`).
- Coarse GitHub date search (`>=2026-06-12`) is **701 / 800 / 9**, but that includes June 12 pre-release items.
- Edge audit subtracts **13 PRs / 17 issues / 1 epic** closed before the release commit timestamp.
- Exact post-cut live count is therefore **688 merged PRs / 783 closed issues / 8 closed epics**.

Current implication for this ticket: stay `not-code-ready` until #14530 merges and #14475 reaches final publish-handoff, but the final sweep must not blindly reuse the local issue mirror. The mirror is 5 closed issues behind live GitHub; confirmed example: `resources/content/issues/chunk-9/issue-14312.md` still says `state: OPEN`, while GitHub closed #14312 at `2026-07-03T03:24:09Z`.

Final pass should either sync content first and regenerate the appendix, or keep local-mirror wording explicit and use exact live GitHub counts for public proof figures.

Release-owner packet: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/14475#issuecomment-4872492955

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-03T04:18:55Z

## Pre-activation command-reference inventory

Read-only prep while #14530 is still in peer review. This does **not** activate the sweep yet; it records the command-ref surface for the final pass.

V-B-A command:

`node --input-type=module -e ...` over `README.md`, `learn/**`, `.github/VISION.md`, and `resources/content/release-notes/v13.1.0.md`, comparing every `npm run <script>` reference against `package.json` scripts.

Result: **149** `npm run` refs checked; missing-script refs grouped by file:

| File | Missing scripts |
|---|---|
| `learn/blog/ai-native-platform-answers-questions.md` | `ai:query`, `ai:build-kb` |
| `learn/agentos/tooling/MemoryCoreMcpApi.md` | `ai:add-memory`, `ai:query-memory`, `ai:summarize-session`, `ai:export-memory`, `ai:import-memory` |
| `learn/guides/testing/Benchmarking.md` | `build:benchmark-app`, `test:*`, `benchmark:accurate:*`, `benchmark:generate-report:*` family refs not present in current `package.json` |
| `learn/agentos/tooling/AiToolingWslSetup.md` | `dev` |

Final-pass caution: this scanner intentionally treats all `npm run` prose the same. The actual #14327 fix needs to classify each hit as live instruction vs historical/blog artifact before editing. Known current replacement direction from this ticket: query/add-memory paths are MCP tools, and `ai:build-kb` should not be presented as the live script (`ai:build-kb-faqs` is the package script, but the prose may need a stronger conceptual rewrite instead of a blind rename).

The key point for release readiness: the dead-reference AC is real and has a bounded hit list ready for the activation pass.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-03T04:19:54Z

## Pre-activation MCP-server count inventory

Read-only prep while #14530 is still in peer review. This records the count source for the final sweep.

V-B-A:

- `package.json` scripts beginning with `ai:mcp-server-`: **5**
  - `ai:mcp-server-file-system`
  - `ai:mcp-server-github-workflow`
  - `ai:mcp-server-knowledge-base`
  - `ai:mcp-server-memory-core`
  - `ai:mcp-server-neural-link`
- `rg --files ai/mcp/server -g mcp-server.mjs`: **6** physical server entrypoints, because it also finds `ai/mcp/server/gitlab-workflow/mcp-server.mjs`.
- ADR-0018 already names the intended distinction: public/counting surface derives from package scripts / audience split, not naive filesystem count.

Candidate prose drift found for the final pass:

- `learn/agentos/tooling/Introduction.md` currently says "four specialized MCP servers".
- `learn/agentos/v13-path.md`, `learn/agentos/tooling/MCPServerBaseClass.md`, README enumeration, and `learn/benefits/ApplicationEngine.md` are aligned with the 5-server package-exposed surface.
- Cloud deployment docs that say **two public MCP servers** appear scope-specific (KB + MC cloud plane), not automatically stale.

Final-pass implication: keep the canonical general count at 5 unless the package script surface changes; do not count the gitlab-workflow PoC as a shipped/public MCP server merely because a filesystem entrypoint exists.

Candidate drift for final pass:

- `README.md` still says **706 merged PRs and 800 closed issues** for May 2026.
- `learn/benefits/Introduction.md` already uses **978 June / 736 May** for merged PRs, so it is closer to current PR-count truth.

Final-pass caution: these are fixed calendar-window counts, not release-window counts. Do not mix them with the v13.1 release-cut count (`688 merged PRs / 783 live closed issues / 8 closed epics` after the v13.0.0 timestamp edge). If updating README, preserve the distinction between monthly proof figures and release-window figures.
